428 J Street. Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660. Fax (916) 322-0886
Nrjl23,2003
Victoria Wasko, CMC
Post Office Box 940
Corona, C492878-0940
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. til-03-057
Dear Ms. Wasko:
You wrote requesting general information on the lawful use of public funds to
provide city residents with educational information about a ballot measure. As we
discussed by phone on Monday, April 2l,many of the legal restrictions concerning the
use of public funds in connection with a ballot measure are outside the Political Reform
Act (the'Act').r
I am enclosing copies of the following items to provide you with general
information concerning restrictions on the use of public funds in connection with a ballot
measure:
o FPPC Bush Advice Letter, No. I-02-015
o Government Code section 54964 (prohibiting officers and employees from
expending funds of a local agency to support or oppose the approval or
rejection of a ballot measure by the voters).
o Attorney General Opinion No. 89-1202,73 Ops. Cal. Atty. Gen. 255
o League of Women Voters v. Countywide Criminal Justice Coordination
Committee,203 Cal. App. 3d 529 (1988)
As we discussed by phone, we will consider your request for formal written
advice withdrawn. Please feel free to give me a call at (916) 327-2020 if you have any
questions after reviewing this material. In addition, if you or the city council has a
specific question conceming the use of public funds in connection with a ballot measure,
please do not hesitate to call or request written advice from the FPPC for questions about
I Government Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
€t


File No. W-03-057
Page No. 2
the Act, or from the Attorney General's Office for questions about other provisions of the
Government Code.
Sincerely,
-61,/*P, LJ
Hyla P. Wagner
Senior Counsel, Legal Division
Enclosures
